message:
test(filesystem): add comprehensive real filesystem operation tests (ntm-vtq7)

Add real file operation tests without mocks:

config/extra_test.go:
- TestInitProjectConfigScaffolding: templates dir, TOML validation, palette content
- TestInitProjectConfigPreservesExistingPalette
- TestInitProjectConfigDirectoryPermissions

util/atomic_test.go (new):
- TestAtomicWriteFile: content, permissions, overwrites, empty, large, binary
- TestAtomicWriteFileCleanupOnError
- TestAtomicWriteFileConcurrent

checkpoint/checkpoint_test.go:
- TestStorage_SaveScrollback_LargeContent (1MB+)
- TestStorage_SaveScrollback_SpecialCharacters (ANSI, Unicode, null bytes)
- TestStorage_FilePermissions
- TestStorage_LoadCorruptedJSON
- TestStorage_MultiplePanes
- TestStorage_EmptyScrollback

Closes: ntm-vtq7, ntm-snzv, ntm-zq4v, ntm-buui

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-bsv4","title":"Implement pipeline notifications","description":"Notify on pipeline completion, failure, or key events. Keeps users informed of workflow progress.

NOTIFICATION EVENTS:
1. Pipeline started
2. Pipeline completed successfully
3. Pipeline failed (step error, timeout)
4. Pipeline cancelled
5. Step completed (optional, verbose mode)
6. Step failed with retry
7. Parallel group completed

NOTIFICATION CHANNELS:

1. DESKTOP NOTIFICATION:
   Same as health alerts - notify-send / osascript

2. WEBHOOK:
   POST to configured URL with event payload

3. AGENT MAIL:
   Send message to user/coordinator
   Thread by pipeline run ID

4. STDOUT PROGRESS:
   For interactive mode, print progress

CONFIGURATION:
```yaml
# In workflow file
settings:
  notify_on_complete: true
  notify_on_error: true
  notify_channels: [desktop, webhook, mail]
  notify_verbose: false  # if true, notify on each step
  
  webhook_url: https://...
  mail_recipient: "user"  # or agent name
```

MESSAGE TEMPLATES:
```
# Success
Pipeline '{name}' completed successfully
Duration: {duration}
Steps: {completed}/{total}

# Failure
Pipeline '{name}' failed at step '{step}'
Error: {error}
Duration before failure: {duration}

# Step failure (with retry)
Step '{step}' failed (attempt {n}/{max})
Error: {error}
Retrying in {delay}...
```

AGENT MAIL FORMAT:
```markdown
Subject: [Pipeline] {name}: {status}

## Pipeline Execution Report

**Status**: {status}
**Duration**: {duration}
**Run ID**: {run_id}

### Steps Completed
{for each completed step}
- ✓ {step.name} ({step.duration})
{end for}

### Errors
{if errors}
- ✗ {step.name}: {error}
{end if}

### Output Summary
{truncated output from final step}
```

LOCATION: internal/pipeline/notify.go (new file)

DEPENDS ON:
- Alerting system (ntm-caib) - for desktop/webhook infrastructure
- Execution engine (ntm-gcgx)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All event types trigger notifications
- All channels work (desktop, webhook, mail)
- Configuration respected
- Messages informative and formatted nicely
- Verbose mode works for step-by-step updates","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:48:48.792831401-06:00","updated_at":"2026-01-06T00:13:18.761856-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T00:13:18.761856-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented notification system with desktop, webhook, and Agent Mail channels. Integrated with executor.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-bsv4","depends_on_id":"ntm-gcgx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:48:55.505307588-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-bsv4","depends_on_id":"ntm-caib","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:48:55.56710323-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-c2b","title":"EPIC: Plugin & Extension System","description":"Allow users to extend NTM with custom agents, commands, and hooks.

## Problem Statement

NTM currently supports three hardcoded agent types: Claude, Codex, Gemini.
Users may want to use other agents or customize behavior:

1. **Other AI Agents**: Cursor, Aider, Continue, OpenAI API, local LLMs
2. **Custom Workflows**: Project-specific commands and automation
3. **Integration Hooks**: Run scripts before/after NTM operations
4. **Team Customization**: Share agent configs across a team

Currently, users must fork NTM to add new agent types or modify behavior.

## Goals

1. Allow custom agent definitions via config files
2. Support custom commands as executable scripts
3. Provide hook system for pre/post command execution
4. Make plugins discoverable and easy to install
5. Document plugin development clearly

## Why This Matters

Extensibility enables:
- Immediate support for any new AI coding agent
- Project-specific workflows without code changes
- Team-shared configurations and standards
- Community plugin ecosystem
- Future-proofing against agent landscape changes

## Success Criteria

- Users can define custom agents in ~/.config/ntm/agents/
- ntm spawn myproject --cursor=2 (custom agent)
- Users can add commands via ~/.config/ntm/commands/
- ntm my-custom-command (runs script)
- Hooks fire at defined extension points
- ntm plugins list shows installed plugins

## Plugin Types

### 1. Agent Plugins (~/.config/ntm/agents/*.toml)

Define new agent types:

# cursor.toml
[agent]
name = "cursor"
alias = "cur"
command = "cursor --headless --ai-mode"
description = "Cursor AI editor"
default_tags = ["cursor", "editor"]

Usage: ntm spawn myproject --cursor=2

### 2. Command Plugins (~/.config/ntm/commands/*)

Executable scripts become commands:

#\!/bin/bash
# ~/.config/ntm/commands/sync-all
# Description: Sync all agents with latest code

for session in $(ntm list --json | jq -r '.[].name'); do
  ntm send "$session" --all "git pull && review changes"
done

Usage: ntm sync-all

### 3. Hook Plugins (~/.config/ntm/hooks/*)

Run before/after NTM commands:

# hooks.toml
[hooks.pre_send]
command = "~/.config/ntm/hooks/log-prompt.sh"
pass_args = true

[hooks.post_spawn]
command = "notify-send 'NTM' 'Session created'"

## Technical Approach

Plugin Discovery:
- Scan known directories on startup
- Cache plugin metadata for performance
- Reload on ntm plugins refresh

Agent Plugin Loading:
- Parse TOML to AgentConfig struct
- Register with spawn/add commands
- Generate shell integration aliases

Command Plugin Execution:
- Discover executables in commands/
- Run with NTM context in environment
- Pass JSON via stdin, capture stdout/stderr

Hook Execution:
- Define extension points in each command
- Run hooks synchronously (pre) or async (post)
- Pass command context as JSON
- Fail-safe: hook errors don't break NTM

## Security Considerations

- Plugins are user-installed, user-trusted
- No automatic plugin download/installation
- Clear documentation of execution context
- Hooks can see all command arguments
- Consider --no-hooks flag for sensitive operations

## Interaction with Other Features

- JSON Output: Plugin commands can use --json
- Config: Plugins extend config schema
- Remote: Plugins work locally, commands sent to remote
- Tags: Custom agents can have default tags

## Future Possibilities

- Plugin registry/marketplace
- Version pinning for plugins
- Plugin dependencies
- Programmatic plugin API (Go plugins)

 {"id":"ntm-c36","title":"Implement --robot-health for agent health matrix","description":"Focused health check returning responsiveness and activity metrics.

## Rationale
--robot-snapshot includes health data, but sometimes I just need a quick health check without the full state. This is the lightweight "are things working" query.

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "checked_at": "ISO8601",
  "system": {
    "tmux_ok": true,
    "disk_free_gb": 42.5,
    "load_avg": 1.2
  },
  "sessions": {
    "myproject": {
      "healthy": true,
      "agents": {
        "0.1": {"responsive": true, "output_rate": "high", "last_activity_sec": 2},
        "0.2": {"responsive": true, "output_rate": "low", "last_activity_sec": 45},
        "0.3": {"responsive": false, "issue": "no_output_5min"}
      }
    }
  },
  "alerts": ["0.3: no output 5min"]
}
```

## Health Heuristics
- responsive: has pane produced output recently AND is tmux pane alive
- output_rate: high (>1 line/sec), medium, low (<1 line/min), none
- last_activity_sec: time since last output line

## Alert Conditions
- No output for 5 minutes (configurable)
- Pane process exited
- High CPU in pane (if detectable)
- Disk space low
- Session unexpectedly killed

## Use Cases
1. Quick sanity check before sending batch instructions
2. Detect stuck agents for restart
3. System health monitoring
4. Pre-flight check before spawning more agents","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackSnow","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:51:22.401882-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T09:26:09.134385-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T09:26:09.134385-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-sim","title":"Implement --robot-snapshot for unified state query","description":"Core orchestration primitive: single command returns complete system state including all sessions, agent health, bead summary, mail status, and alerts. This is THE most important flag because it eliminates the need for multiple queries.

## Rationale
When orchestrating N agents across M sessions working on P beads with Q mail threads, I currently need separate queries for each dimension. This compounds latency (4-6 tool calls) and burns tokens on redundant parsing. A unified snapshot gives me complete situational awareness in one atomic read.

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "ts": "ISO8601",
  "sessions": [{
    "name": "string",
    "agents": [{
      "pane": "window.pane",
      "type": "claude|codex|gemini|cursor|windsurf|aider",
      "state": "active|idle|stuck|waiting",
      "last_output_age_sec": number,
      "output_tail_lines": number,
      "current_bead": "bead-id|null",
      "pending_mail": number
    }]
  }],
  "beads_summary": {"open": N, "in_progress": N, "blocked": N, "ready": N},
  "mail_unread": number,
  "alerts": ["string"]
}
```

## Key Design Decisions
- Always include everything (no optional fields that require follow-up)
- Use compact field names (abbreviations OK for frequently-accessed data)
- Include computed fields (last_output_age_sec vs raw timestamp)
- Alerts are pre-filtered to actionable items only

## Integration Points
- tmux: session/pane enumeration, output capture
- beads: bd list --json integration  
- agent-mail: inbox count per agent
- pane title parsing for agent type detection","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:07.458116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:34.255328-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:34.255328-05:00","comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"ntm-sim","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Implementation Architecture

### File Structure
```
internal/robot/
├── robot.go          # Existing - add Snapshot type and function
├── snapshot.go       # NEW - SnapshotOutput, BuildSnapshot()
├── integrations.go   # NEW - BeadsIntegration, MailIntegration
└── alerts.go         # NEW - AlertManager, Alert types
```

### Core Type
```go
type SnapshotOutput struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time              `json:"ts"`
    Sessions    []SessionSnapshot      `json:"sessions"`
    Beads       *BeadsSnapshot         `json:"beads,omitempty"`
    AgentMail   *MailSnapshot          `json:"agent_mail,omitempty"`
    Alerts      []Alert                `json:"alerts"`
    Summary     SnapshotSummary        `json:"summary"`
}
```

### Flag Registration
In root.go init():
```go
rootCmd.Flags().Bool("robot-snapshot", false, "Output complete system state as JSON")
rootCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Return only changes since timestamp (ISO8601)")
```

### Performance Target
- Cold snapshot: <100ms
- With caching: <20ms
- Memory: <5MB for 10 sessions with 50 panes","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:55:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-sk07","title":"Feature: CASS Historical Context Injection","description":"# CASS Historical Context Injection

## The Opportunity
CASS indexes 50K+ sessions across 11 different agent types (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Roo, Cline, Windsurf, etc.) with sub-60ms search.

NTM could inject relevant historical context before spawning agents, so they don't reinvent solutions.

## CASS Capabilities
- Multi-agent indexing (11 agent types)
- Full-text search
- Semantic search (embedding-based)
- Hybrid search (combined)
- Multi-machine unified index

## Implementation (internal/context/historical.go)

### Search Function
```go
type CASSMatch struct {
    SessionID   string
    AgentType   string
    Timestamp   time.Time
    Score       float64
    Snippet     string
    FilesEdited []string
}

func searchHistoricalContext(task string, limit int) ([]CASSMatch, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("cass", "search",
        "--query", task,
        "--limit", fmt.Sprintf("%d", limit),
        "--mode", "hybrid",
        "--json",
    )
    // Graceful degradation if CASS unavailable
}
```

### Context Pack Integration
CASS results included in the ContextPack built before task assignment:
- Budget: 15% of total context budget
- Truncated if exceeds budget
- Cached by (repo_rev, task, limit)

## CASS Commands
```bash
cass search "authentication error" --robot --limit 5
cass view /path/to/session.jsonl -n 42 --json
cass expand /path/to/session.jsonl -n 42 -C 3 --json
```

## New NTM Commands
```bash
ntm context history "implement JWT auth"
ntm context history --agent=claude --days=7
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CASS search wrapper implemented
- [ ] Results included in ContextPack
- [ ] Token budget enforced (15%)
- [ ] Graceful degradation if CASS unavailable
- [ ] Filters: agent type, recency","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:18:57.5946-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T20:18:57.5946-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-sk07","depends_on_id":"ntm-dwuv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:19:56.12452-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sk07","depends_on_id":"ntm-6hxh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:19:57.022706-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-sor1","title":"Feature: File Reservation Lifecycle","description":"# File Reservation Lifecycle

## The Problem
NTM spawns multiple agents on the same codebase with NO file coordination. Result: merge conflicts, lost work, frustrated developers.

## The Solution
Advisory file locks via Agent Mail:
1. RESERVE before assigning work
2. RENEW heartbeat while working
3. RELEASE after completion
4. FORCE-RELEASE if agent crashes (with approval)

## Lifecycle
```
Reserve → Work → Release
    ↓
  Conflict? → Find alternative work OR wait
    ↓
  Stale? → Force-release (requires approval)
```

## Implementation

### Reserve Before Assignment
```go
func assignWorkWithReservations(ctx, session, agent, bead) {
    files := predictAffectedFiles(bead)
    reservations := agentmail.ReservePaths(ctx, files)
    if len(reservations.Conflicts) > 0 {
        // Find alternative work or wait
    }
    // Assign work
}
```

### Predict Affected Files
Use bv --robot-impact if available, otherwise use label-to-pattern mapping.

### Release After Completion
```go
func OnTaskComplete(ctx, session, agentName) {
    agentmail.ReleaseReservations(ctx, projectPath, agentName)
}
```

### Force-Release Stale (with Approval)
```go
func checkForStaleReservations(ctx) {
    for _, res := range reservations {
        if time.Since(agent.LastActiveTS) > staleTimeout {
            approval := policyChecker.CheckApproval(ctx, "force_release", res.ID)
            if approval.Granted {
                agentmail.ForceReleaseReservation(ctx, res.ID)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Pre-Commit Guards
```bash
ntm guards install    # Install Agent Mail pre-commit hook
ntm guards status     # Check hook status
```

## New Commands
```bash
ntm reserve "internal/auth/**/*.go" --agent=GreenLake --ttl=1h
ntm release --agent=GreenLake
ntm reservations list
ntm reservations force-release <id> --reason="Agent crashed"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reserve-before-assign implemented
- [ ] Release-after-complete implemented
- [ ] Force-release with approval workflow
- [ ] Pre-commit guard installation
- [ ] Conflicts detected and handled gracefully","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:40.737974-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T12:53:26.805914-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T12:53:26.805914-05:00","close_reason":"File Reservation Lifecycle implemented: ntm lock/unlock/locks commands with force-release and renew subcommands. Pre-commit guards already existed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-xz26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:52.39541-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp4g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:52.935065-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-6g41","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:53.112053-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-014q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:27:28.488802-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-gy4o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:28:11.837956-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-tvux","title":"Implement ntm checkpoint command","description":"# Implement ntm checkpoint Command

Create named checkpoints with optional description.

 {"id":"ntm-twff","title":"Feature: Human Notification Routing","description":"# Human Notification Routing (internal/notify/)

## Why This Is Critical
Multiple features send messages to "Human" (a reserved Agent Mail identity):
- Session Coordinator: Periodic digests
- Approval Workflow: Pending approval requests
- UBS: Critical bug notifications
- Alerts: System alerts

But there's no configuration for HOW humans actually receive these notifications.

## Notification Channels
Humans can receive notifications via:
1. **Desktop notifications** (system native)
2. **Slack** (webhook or app)
3. **Email** (SMTP)
4. **Telegram** (bot API)
5. **File drop** (.ntm/human_inbox/ for offline review)
6. **Agent Mail** (if human has an agent identity)

## Configuration (.ntm/config.yaml)
```yaml
notifications:
  human:
    # Primary channel
    primary: slack
    
    # Fallback if primary fails
    fallback: file
    
    # Channel configurations
    channels:
      slack:
        webhook_url: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}
        channel: "#ntm-alerts"
        
      email:
        smtp_host: smtp.gmail.com
        smtp_port: 587
        from: ntm@example.com
        to: developer@example.com
        
      telegram:
        bot_token: ${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}
        chat_id: ${TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID}
        
      desktop:
        enabled: true
        sound: true
        
      file:
        path: .ntm/human_inbox/
        
    # Routing rules by notification type
    routing:
      digest: [file]           # Low urgency - just log it
      approval: [slack, desktop]  # High urgency - multiple channels
      alert: [slack, desktop, telegram]  # Critical - all channels
      bug: [slack]             # Medium urgency
```

## Implementation (internal/notify/router.go)

### Core Types
```go
type Notifier struct {
    config   *NotifyConfig
    channels map[string]Channel
}

type Channel interface {
    Name() string
    Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) error
    Available() bool
}

type Message struct {
    Type       string    // "digest", "approval", "alert", "bug"
    Subject    string
    Body       string
    Urgency    string    // "low", "normal", "high", "critical"
    Metadata   map[string]string
    CreatedAt  time.Time
}

// Concrete implementations
type SlackChannel struct { ... }
type EmailChannel struct { ... }
type TelegramChannel struct { ... }
type DesktopChannel struct { ... }
type FileChannel struct { ... }
```

### Routing Logic
```go
func (n *Notifier) Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) error {
    // 1. Get channels for this message type
    channels := n.config.GetChannels(msg.Type)
    if len(channels) == 0 {
        channels = []string{n.config.Primary}
    }
    
    // 2. Try each channel in order
    var lastErr error
    for _, chName := range channels {
        ch := n.channels[chName]
        if ch == nil || !ch.Available() {
            continue
        }
        
        if err := ch.Send(ctx, msg); err != nil {
            lastErr = err
            continue
        }
        
        // Success on at least one channel
        return nil
    }
    
    // 3. Fallback
    if lastErr != nil && n.config.Fallback != "" {
        if fb := n.channels[n.config.Fallback]; fb != nil {
            return fb.Send(ctx, msg)
        }
    }
    
    return lastErr
}
```

### Desktop Notifications
Uses OS-native notification system:
```go
func (d *DesktopChannel) Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) error {
    switch runtime.GOOS {
    case "darwin":
        // osascript -e 'display notification ...'
        return exec.Command("osascript", "-e",
            fmt.Sprintf(`display notification "%s" with title "%s"`,
                msg.Body, msg.Subject)).Run()
    case "linux":
        // notify-send
        return exec.Command("notify-send", msg.Subject, msg.Body).Run()
    }
    return nil
}
```

### File Channel
Creates markdown files for offline review:
```go
func (f *FileChannel) Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) error {
    filename := fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s.md", 
        msg.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02_15-04-05"),
        msg.Type)
    path := filepath.Join(f.path, filename)
    
    content := fmt.Sprintf("# %s\n\n%s\n\n---\nUrgency: %s\nTime: %s\n",
        msg.Subject, msg.Body, msg.Urgency, msg.CreatedAt)
    
    return os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644)
}
```

## Integration Points
- Session Coordinator: Uses for digest summaries
- Approval Workflow: Uses for pending approvals
- UBS: Uses for bug notifications
- Event Bus: Subscribe to events and notify

## New Commands
```bash
ntm notify test slack          # Test Slack channel
ntm notify test desktop        # Test desktop notification
ntm notify config show         # Show notification config
ntm notify inbox               # Show file inbox
ntm notify inbox --clear       # Clear file inbox
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multiple channels implemented (Slack, Email, Desktop, File)
- [ ] Routing rules per message type
- [ ] Primary/fallback channel logic
- [ ] Environment variable expansion in config
- [ ] Graceful degradation when channel unavailable
- [ ] Test command for each channel","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:29:49.63589-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T03:31:21.488974-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T03:31:21.488974-05:00","close_reason":"Human Notification Routing implemented with: (1) Multiple channels - Desktop, Webhook, Shell, Log, FileBox (file inbox for offline review); (2) Routing rules per event type via config.Routing map; (3) Primary/fallback channel logic - tries channels in order, stops on first success; (4) Environment variable expansion via os.ExpandEnv for URLs, paths, headers; (5) Graceful degradation - errors collected per channel without blocking others; (6) All 11 tests passing including new tests for FileBox, Routing, PrimaryFallback, EnvVarExpansion.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-twff","depends_on_id":"ntm-wotk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:29:58.878698-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-twff","depends_on_id":"ntm-mzsq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:30:42.103696-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"ntm-txio","title":"Add checkpoint export/import for sharing","description":"## Overview
Export checkpoints as portable archives for sharing and backup.

## Location
Create `internal/checkpoint/export.go`

## Export Command
```bash
ntm checkpoint export <name> --output=checkpoint.tar.gz
ntm checkpoint export <name> --output=checkpoint.zip
ntm checkpoint export <name> --redact-secrets  # Remove sensitive data
```

## Archive Contents
```
checkpoint.tar.gz/
  checkpoint.json
  scrollback/
    cc_1.txt.gz
    cc_2.txt.gz
  metadata/
    git_diff.patch
    beads_state.json
  MANIFEST.txt  # List of files with checksums
```

## Import Command
```bash
ntm checkpoint import checkpoint.tar.gz
ntm checkpoint import checkpoint.tar.gz --name=imported-session
ntm checkpoint import checkpoint.tar.gz --project=/path/to/project
```

## Path Rewriting on Import
```go
func RewritePaths(checkpoint *Checkpoint, newProjectRoot string) {
    // Update project_root
    // Adjust relative paths
    // Warn about git remote differences
}
```

Before export, optionally redact:
- API keys in scrollback
- Tokens in environment
- Credentials in config

```go
}
```

 {"id":"ntm-vr2","title":"SUB-EPIC: E2E Tests with Detailed Logging","description":"# SUB-EPIC: End-to-End (E2E) Tests with Detailed Logging

## Scope
Complete workflow tests that exercise NTM from CLI invocation to tmux state verification.

## E2E Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Full Agent Workflow
1. ntm spawn project --cc=2 --cod=1
2. Verify session created with 4 panes (3 agents + 1 user)
3. ntm send project --cc 'hello world'
4. Verify prompt delivered to Claude panes only
5. ntm interrupt project
6. Verify Ctrl+C sent
7. ntm kill -f project
8. Verify session destroyed

### Scenario 2: Command Palette Workflow
1. Create session with agents
2. Invoke palette (programmatic, not TUI)
3. Select command
4. Verify prompt sent to targets

### Scenario 3: Quick Setup Workflow
1. ntm quick testproject --template=go
2. Verify directory structure created
3. Verify git initialized
4. Verify .vscode and .claude dirs created

### Scenario 4: Session Persistence
1. Create session, add agents
2. Detach
3. Re-attach
4. Verify state preserved

### Scenario 5: Robot Mode Output
1. Create various session states
2. Run --robot-status, --robot-plan
3. Verify JSON output matches actual state
4. Parse and validate JSON schema

## Logging Requirements

### Per-Test Log File
```
test_logs/
├── TestFullAgentWorkflow_20251210_103045.log
├── TestCommandPaletteWorkflow_20251210_103047.log
└── ...
```

### Log Content Structure
```
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] TEST START: TestFullAgentWorkflow
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] SETUP: Creating test session 'ntm_test_abc123'
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] EXEC: ntm spawn ntm_test_abc123 --cc=2 --cod=1
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] STDOUT: Session created: ntm_test_abc123 (4 panes)
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] VERIFY: Checking pane count...
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] ASSERT PASS: pane_count == 4
[2025-12-10T10:30:46Z] EXEC: ntm send ntm_test_abc123 --cc 'hello'
...
[2025-12-10T10:30:48Z] TEARDOWN: Killing session ntm_test_abc123
[2025-12-10T10:30:48Z] TEST END: PASS (3.2s)
```

### System State Capture
Before and after each test:
- tmux list-sessions
- tmux list-panes (for test session)
- Environment variables
- Working directory

## Implementation
- tests/e2e/ directory for all E2E tests
- Shared test harness with logging
- CI-compatible (skip if tmux unavailable)
- Parallel execution with unique session names","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:52.553331-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T12:21:24.959947-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T22:37:49.867691-05:00","close_reason":"E2E test framework and tests implemented: agent_workflow_test.go (5 tests), quick_test.go (8 tests), robot_mode_test.go (9 tests). All passing with detailed logging via testutil/multi.go","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-vr2","depends_on_id":"ntm-l5h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:06.044553-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-vr2","depends_on_id":"ntm-7lk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:06.232148-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-vum","title":"EPIC: MCP Agent Mail Integration","description":"# MCP Agent Mail Integration

## Background & Motivation

NTM orchestrates multiple AI coding agents (Claude, Codex, Gemini) in parallel tmux panes. Currently, these agents operate in isolation - they can see the same filesystem but cannot:
- Communicate with each other directly
- Know what others are working on
- Coordinate file access to avoid conflicts
- Share status or hand off work

MCP Agent Mail provides exactly this coordination layer.

## CRITICAL CLARIFICATION: NTM's Role

**NTM is a COORDINATOR and BRIDGE, not an agent replacement.**

The agents running in panes (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) may or may not have their own Agent Mail integration:
- If they DO have Agent Mail support: NTM provides unified visibility (aggregate inbox, lock status)
- If they DON'T: NTM acts as coordinator, sending messages on behalf of the user

**Key insight**: NTM doesn't pretend to BE the agents. It's the orchestration layer that:
1. Provides a unified view of all agent mail activity
2. Allows the USER to send messages to/between agents
3. Shows file reservation status across all agents
4. Detects conflicts before spawning new agents

## What NTM's Agent Mail Integration Provides

### 1. Visibility Layer
- Aggregate inbox view: See messages across all registered agents
- File lock status: Who has reserved what files
- Agent directory: Which agents are active in the project

### 2. User-to-Agent Communication
```bash
ntm mail send myproject --to cc_1 "Please focus on the API endpoint"
```
This sends FROM the NTM coordinator TO the specified agent. It's the user speaking through NTM.

### 3. Session Coordination
```bash
ntm lock myproject "src/api/**" --reason "User-directed refactor"
```
NTM can reserve files on behalf of the session, signaling to all agents that these files are being coordinated.

### 4. Conflict Prevention
```bash
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2
# Warning: src/api/** is reserved by GreenCastle (cc_1 in session 'other')
# Proceed? [y/N]
```

## Technical Approach

MCP Agent Mail runs as an HTTP server (default port 8765) with JSON-RPC 2.0.

NTM will:
1. **Create internal/agentmail package**: HTTP client for Agent Mail MCP tools
2. **Register as coordinator**: `ntm_<session>` identity for user-initiated actions
3. **Aggregate views**: Fetch inbox/locks from all known agents
4. **Expose commands**: ntm mail, ntm lock, ntm locks
5. **Dashboard integration**: Show mail and lock status
6. **Graceful degradation**: All features optional if Agent Mail unavailable

## Key Design Decisions

- **NTM is a coordinator**: Sends messages as `ntm_<session>`, not as individual agents
- **Agents own their mail**: If Claude Code has Agent Mail support, it handles its own inbox
- **NTM aggregates and visualizes**: Unified view across all project agents
- **Optional integration**: Works without Agent Mail; features degrade gracefully
- **No duplication**: Don't re-implement what agents already do

## What This Is NOT

- NOT a replacement for agent-side Agent Mail integration
- NOT pretending NTM "is" the Claude/Codex/Gemini agent
- NOT required for basic NTM functionality

## Success Criteria

- [ ] NTM can detect if Agent Mail server is running
- [ ] NTM registers coordinator identity per session
- [ ] Aggregate inbox shows messages across all project agents
- [ ] File reservation status visible in status/dashboard
- [ ] Conflicts detected before spawning in reserved areas
- [ ] Graceful degradation when Agent Mail unavailable","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:17:05.78207-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T12:21:24.960288-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T23:08:41.113882-05:00","close_reason":"Agent Mail integration complete: internal/agentmail package (client, session, tools, types), CLI commands (mail send/inbox/read/ack, lock/locks), robot mode (--robot-mail), dashboard integration, graceful degradation when unavailable. 31.5% test coverage."}
 {"id":"ntm-vz9","title":"Implement ntm copy command with filtering","description":"Copy pane content to system clipboard with filtering options.

## What This Task Does

Create 'ntm copy' command to copy pane output to system clipboard.

## Interface

# Copy entire pane output
$ ntm copy myproject:1

# Copy last N lines
$ ntm copy myproject:1 --last 50

# Copy lines matching pattern
$ ntm copy myproject:1 --pattern 'ERROR.*'

# Copy only code blocks (markdown fences)
$ ntm copy myproject:1 --code

# Copy to file instead of clipboard
$ ntm copy myproject:1 --output result.txt

# Quiet mode (no confirmation message)
$ ntm copy myproject:1 --quiet

## Content Filtering

--last N: Capture last N lines of pane output
--pattern: Filter to lines matching regex
--code: Extract markdown code blocks only

Code block extraction:
```python
def hello():
    print('world')
```

Becomes:
def hello():
    print('world')

Multiple code blocks concatenated with newlines.

## Implementation

1. Capture pane content (existing CapturePaneOutput)
2. Apply filters in order:
   a. --last (if specified)
   b. --pattern (if specified)
   c. --code (if specified)
3. Copy to clipboard or file
4. Show confirmation unless --quiet

## JSON Output

$ ntm copy myproject:1 --json
{
  "source": "myproject:1",

 {"id":"ntm-zky5","title":"Feature: Remaining BV Robot Modes","description":"# Remaining BV Robot Modes

## The Opportunity
bv has 41 robot modes but NTM only uses 4. The remaining 33+ provide valuable analysis.

## Currently Used (4)
- -robot-insights
- -robot-priority
- -robot-plan
- -robot-recipes

## Unused Modes to Integrate

### Analysis Modes
- -robot-alerts: Proactive issue detection (cycles, stale, orphans)
- -robot-impact: File impact analysis for reservations
- -robot-forecast: ETA predictions
- -robot-causality: Causal chain analysis
- -robot-suggest: Smart suggestions
- -robot-search: Semantic vector search
- -robot-capacity: Team capacity simulation

### Label Modes
- -robot-label-attention: Label priority ranking
- -robot-label-flow: Cross-label dependencies
- -robot-label-health: Per-label health metrics

### File Modes
- -robot-file-beads: File-to-bead mapping
- -robot-file-hotspots: Frequently changed files
- -robot-file-relations: Files that change together

### History Modes
- -robot-history: Bead-to-commit correlations
- -robot-orphans: Orphan commits
- -robot-diff: Changes since ref

### Sprint Modes
- -robot-sprint-list: Available sprints
- -robot-sprint-show: Sprint details
- -robot-burndown: Sprint burndown

## Implementation (internal/bv/)
Each mode gets a wrapper function:
```go
func GetAlerts(ctx context.Context, severity string) ([]Alert, error)
func GetImpact(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*ImpactResult, error)
func GetLabelHealth(ctx context.Context) (*LabelHealthResult, error)
```

## New Commands
```bash
ntm work alerts --critical-only
ntm work impact internal/auth/*.go
ntm work labels --health
ntm work history bd-123
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Alert monitoring integrated
- [ ] File impact for reservation prediction
- [ ] Label health visible
- [ ] Sprint tracking if sprints exist
- [ ] Graceful degradation for unsupported modes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:19:44.194343-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T20:19:44.194343-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-zky5","depends_on_id":"ntm-dwuv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:19:56.664992-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-zky5","depends_on_id":"ntm-w4ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:19:57.55868-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-zqt9","title":"Emit rotation events to alert system","description":"## Overview
Integrate context rotation with the alert system for visibility.

## Location
Extend `internal/alerts/`

## Alert Types
```go
// Warning when approaching threshold
AlertContextWarning = AlertType{
    Level:   LevelWarn,
    Icon:    "⚠️",
    Message: "Agent %s context at %d%% - rotation soon",
}

// Info when rotation starts
AlertRotationStarted = AlertType{
    Level:   LevelInfo,
    Icon:    "🔄",
    Message: "Rotating agent %s (context at %d%%)",
}

// Success when rotation completes
AlertRotationComplete = AlertType{
    Level:   LevelSuccess,
    Icon:    "✅",
    Message: "Agent %s rotated to %s successfully",
}

// Error if rotation fails
AlertRotationFailed = AlertType{
    Level:   LevelError,
    Icon:    "❌",
    Message: "Failed to rotate agent %s: %v",
}
```

## Robot Mode Output
```json
{
  "type": "context_rotation",
  "old_agent": "cc_1",
  "new_agent": "cc_1",  
  "usage_percent": 95.2,
  "summary_tokens": 1842,
  "status": "completed"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Warning alerts at warning_threshold
- [ ] Info alert when rotation starts
- [ ] Success/error on completion
- [ ] Robot mode JSON output
- [ ] Alerts appear in dashboard panel","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:12:53.43440608-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:12:53.43440608-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-zqt9","depends_on_id":"ntm-jv8p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:13:06.6680268-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-zqt9","depends_on_id":"ntm-cj0w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:13:08.663563662-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-zqt9","depends_on_id":"ntm-caib","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T21:18:50.463041142-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-ztvw","title":"EPIC: Auto Account Rotation & Quota Management System","description":"# EPIC: Auto Account Rotation & Quota Management System

## Problem Statement
User has "all you can eat" AI plans (Claude Max, GPT Pro, Gemini Ultra) with 5-hour usage limits per account. They have 5 accounts per provider and need to switch when hitting limits.

**Current pain**: Manual rotation is tedious - detecting limits, remembering which account to use, typing commands, completing browser auth, sending continuation prompts.

## Solution Overview (Revised)
A simple, incremental approach that delivers value early:

### Phase 1: Core Rotation Command (THE MVP) - `ntm-cs4z`
The `ntm rotate` command that does the actual work:
- **Restart strategy** (default): Terminate session → prompt for browser switch → start fresh
- **Re-auth strategy** (--preserve-context): Send /login → detect auth success → send continuation
- Claude-only initially (the primary use case)

### Phase 2: Account Configuration - `ntm-qisk`
Simple config for known accounts:
```toml
[[rotation.accounts]]
provider = "claude"
email = "backup1@gmail.com"
alias = "backup1"
```
Enables `ntm rotate` to suggest which account to switch to.

### Phase 3: Auto-Trigger on Rate Limit - `ntm-t7ol`
Hook into existing health detection to automatically prompt for rotation when limits hit.
Leverages existing `internal/health/health.go` patterns.

### Phase 4: Multi-Provider Support - `ntm-2th5`
Add Codex and Gemini support. Note: Codex always uses restart strategy (no in-session re-auth).

### Phase 5: Quota Display (Optional) - `ntm-l6pk`
`ntm quota` command and dashboard integration. Nice-to-have polish, not essential.

### Phase 6: Advanced Features (Future) - `ntm-dj95`
- Multi-pane batch rotation
- Account state persistence
- Auto-rotation without prompts
- TUI rotation wizard

## Key Design Decisions

### Restart > Re-auth (Often)
New sessions auto-use the browser's current Google account. The restart flow is:
1. Quit session
2. User switches browser account (no /login needed!)
3. Start new session

This is often simpler and faster than re-auth, especially near compaction.

### No PTY Library Needed
Use tmux operations we already have:
- `tmux.SendKeys()` to send commands
- `tmux.CapturePaneOutput()` to check results

### Leverage Existing Infrastructure
- Rate limit detection: `internal/health/health.go` already has patterns
- Notifications: `internal/notify/notify.go` already has EventRateLimit
- Resilience: `internal/resilience/monitor.go` already tracks RateLimited state

## What We're NOT Building (Vaporware Prevention)
- Strategy selection based on "token usage" - Claude doesn't expose this
- Strategy selection based on "compaction timing" - Not externally detectable
- Fully automatic rotation without any user interaction - Too risky

## Starting Point
`ntm-cs4z` (Phase 1) is the foundation. Start there.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:12:45.072486-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T17:36:53.115503-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T17:36:53.115503-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ztvw","depends_on_id":"ntm-cs4z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:35:21.109458-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ztvw","depends_on_id":"ntm-qisk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:35:21.197873-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ztvw","depends_on_id":"ntm-t7ol","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:35:21.32675-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ztvw","depends_on_id":"ntm-2th5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:35:21.671727-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ztvw","depends_on_id":"ntm-l6pk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:35:21.806086-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ztvw","depends_on_id":"ntm-dj95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:35:21.893395-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
